meta.title: SG-SCD-2: Reusable Modeling, Embedding, and Governed Performance

questions.stem: Which TWO steps are most likely to improve a slow Sigma workbook? (Select TWO)

options.text: Duplicate the workbook into more folders

questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because early filtering and selective materialization are common performance improvements. **C** is incorrect because controls can be useful and disabling them blindly does not address root query cost. **D** is incorrect because folder duplication does not optimize execution. [Materialization](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/materialization-overview)

questions.stem: Which Sigma feature supports manual business-user writeback to the connected warehouse?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because input tables are designed for governed manual data entry and writeback use cases. **B** is incorrect because controls filter data rather than storing manual records. **C** is incorrect because API tokens authenticate automation. **D** is incorrect because version tags track content versions. [Input tables](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/input-tables-overview)

questions.stem: Why should embed tokens include an expiry?

options.text: To limit the window in which the embedded authorization can be misused

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because expiration reduces risk from leaked or reused embed tokens. **B** is incorrect because it does not affect chart aesthetics. **C** is incorrect because input tables and datasets are separate features. **D** is incorrect because not applicable. [Embed analytics with Sigma](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/embed-analytics-overview)

questions.stem: A user should be able to explore and build ad hoc analyses but not administer the entire organization. Which Sigma role is the closest fit in this scenario?

options.text: Explorer or Creator rather than Admin

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because Explorer/Creator roles support analysis and content creation without organization-wide administration. **B** is incorrect because Admin is broader than required. **C** is incorrect because every user needs some role/access pattern. **D** is incorrect because Viewer is too limited for authoring. [Permissions and sharing](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/share-content-and-manage-permissions)

questions.stem: Which calculation type is best for running totals or rankings within Sigma?

options.text: Window functions such as running average or rank

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because running totals and rankings are window-style analytical calculations. **B** is incorrect because JWTs, credentials, and roles are unrelated to analytic formulas. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [Workbooks overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/workbooks-overview)

questions.stem: Which option describes the main purpose of user attributes in Sigma security design?

options.text: To support dynamic row-level security and personalization

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because user attributes can drive dynamic filtering and tenant isolation logic. **B** is incorrect because they do not store source data. **C** is incorrect because they complement rather than replace controls. **D** is incorrect because they are not API throttling settings. [User attributes](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/user-attributes)

questions.stem: Which approach is used for client-side embedded Sigma analytics?

options.text: Generate a signed JWT-based embed URL
Open a direct SSH tunnel from the browser
Convert the workbook to CSV first

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because Sigma embedding commonly uses signed JWT URLs or tokens to authorize embedded access. **B** is incorrect because SSH is unrelated. **C** is incorrect because materialization is optional performance tuning. **D** is incorrect because CSV conversion is unnecessary. [Embed analytics with Sigma](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/embed-analytics-overview)

questions.stem: A developer needs a toggle that switches a chart between Gross Margin and Revenue. Which Sigma feature is the most appropriate fit in this scenario?

options.text: A workbook control tied to the chart logic

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because controls are the right interactive mechanism for switching parameters or views. **B** is incorrect because input tables store writeback data. **C** is incorrect because roles and secrets are unrelated to interactivity. [Workbook controls](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/workbook-controls)

questions.stem: Which function family is best for replacing nulls with fallback values?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because functions like `Coalesce` and `IsNull` handle null-related logic. **B** is incorrect because the other choices are not Sigma calculation families. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [Workbooks overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/workbooks-overview)

questions.stem: Which statement most appropriate describes Sigma query execution?

options.text: Sigma attempts to push work down as warehouse-native SQL
Sigma always exports data to spreadsheets first
Sigma stores every query result permanently in browser memory
Sigma requires Python for all calculations

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because Sigma is designed to compile operations into warehouse-native queries where possible. **B** is incorrect because it does not export to spreadsheets first. **C** is incorrect because browser memory is not the permanent execution engine. **D** is incorrect because Python is not required for all calculations. [Workbooks overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/workbooks-overview)

questions.stem: A company wants separate visibility by customer account inside the same workbook. Which approach is most scalable?

options.text: Use row-level filtering driven by user attributes
Clone the workbook for every user manually
Convert the workbook to static PDF

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because user-attribute-driven filtering scales better than maintaining separate copies per user. **B** is incorrect because manual cloning is hard to govern. **C** is incorrect because PDFs are static. **D** is incorrect because version history is unrelated to runtime visibility. [User attributes](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/user-attributes)

questions.stem: A workbook uses complex calculations across the same billions-row dataset every morning. Which feature is most likely to reduce repeated runtime cost in this scenario?

options.text: Turn every control into free text

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because materialization can persist costly intermediate results as warehouse tables for reuse. **B** is incorrect because extra pages do not optimize queries. **C** is incorrect because free-text controls may worsen usability. **D** is incorrect because disabling cache is counterproductive. [Materialization](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/materialization-overview)

questions.stem: Which TWO capabilities make input tables useful for planning workflows? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because input tables support controlled manual entry and writeback patterns for planning or adjustments. **C** is incorrect because JWT generation is unrelated. **D** is incorrect because deleting history is not a feature goal of input tables. [Input tables](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/input-tables-overview)

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about embedding Sigma are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: Signed JWTs can authorize embedded access
Embed parameters can pass dynamic context such as tenant filters
Embedding requires materialization of every workbook
Embedding removes the need for permission design

questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because signed embed authorization and dynamic parameters are common embedded analytics patterns in Sigma. **C** is incorrect because materialization is optional and performance-related, not mandatory for embedding. **D** is incorrect because embedding still requires correct permission and security design. [Embed analytics with Sigma](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/embed-analytics-overview)

questions.stem: Why is query pushdown important in Sigma?

options.text: It lets the warehouse do the heavy computation close to the data
It copies every table into the browser
It removes the need for joins

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because pushdown uses the warehouse engine efficiently. **B** is incorrect because Sigma still generates SQL. **C** is incorrect because tables are not copied wholesale into the browser. **D** is incorrect because joins remain relevant. [Materialization](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/materialization-overview)

questions.stem: Why might a project team publish a Sigma dataset in this scenario?

options.text: To create a reusable governed semantic layer for many workbooks
To convert every workbook into an API

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because published datasets support reuse and governance across workbooks. **B** is incorrect because Sigma still queries the warehouse. **C** is incorrect because joins remain available. **D** is incorrect because datasets are not the same as APIs. [Datasets overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/create-a-dataset)

questions.stem: What is the main advantage of Sigma's visual join builder?

options.text: It lets users define join types and conditions without hand-writing SQL
It physically denormalizes all tables automatically

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because the visual join builder makes join configuration approachable while still pushing SQL to the warehouse. **B** is incorrect because it does not force denormalization. **C** is incorrect because it is not an encryption feature. **D** is incorrect because permissions are managed separately. [Join data in Sigma](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/join-data)

questions.stem: What should be rotated periodically for connections that use service-account credentials?

options.text: The credentials or secrets for the service account

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because credential rotation is a standard operational security practice. **B** is incorrect because tabs, rows, and controls are unrelated to secret hygiene. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [Permissions and sharing](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/share-content-and-manage-permissions)

questions.stem: Which action best reduces workbook latency when users regularly open a page with a highly selective control?

options.text: Apply the control-driven filter as early as possible

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because early selective filtering reduces processed data volume and improves performance. **B** is incorrect because extra visuals add cost. **C** is incorrect because datasets are usually beneficial. **D** is incorrect because string conversion is unrelated. [Workbook controls](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/workbook-controls)

questions.stem: Which scenario is a good fit for an input table in this scenario?

options.text: A sales manager manually enters quarterly targets that should be stored back in the warehouse
A developer wants to define JWT claims
An analyst wants to hide a column with OLS
An admin wants to rotate OAuth secrets

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because input tables support governed manual writeback scenarios like planning or commentary. **B** is incorrect because JWT claims are for embedding. **C** is incorrect because Sigma uses permission and filtering controls rather than OLS terminology here. **D** is incorrect because secret rotation is an admin operation. [Input tables](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/input-tables-overview)

questions.stem: A workbook is slow because a massive join happens before any filters are applied. What is the best first optimization?

options.text: Filter earlier to reduce rows before joining
Add more pages to the workbook

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because reducing data volume before joins usually lowers query cost and improves pushdown efficiency. **B** is incorrect because extra pages do not optimize execution. **C** is incorrect because controls can help rather than hurt. **D** is incorrect because converting measures to text is unrelated. [Materialization](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/materialization-overview)

questions.stem: Which TWO controls are most relevant when securing multi-tenant analytics in Sigma? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because user attributes enable dynamic filtering and granular sharing controls who can access the asset. **C** is incorrect because themes are cosmetic and do not secure data. **D** is incorrect because version tags help release management rather than runtime data security. [User attributes](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/user-attributes)

questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of a Sigma workbook?

options.text: A collaborative analytical document containing pages and data elements

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because a Sigma workbook is the core collaborative surface for analysis and presentation. **B** is incorrect because warehouse drivers connect to data sources but are not the analytical document. **C** is incorrect because Sigma does not replace underlying warehouse storage. **D** is incorrect because it is not an OS package. [Workbooks overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/workbooks-overview)

questions.stem: Which option describes the difference between workspace permissions and workbook permissions in this scenario?

options.text: Workspace permissions can grant broader access across content, while workbook permissions target specific assets
Workbook permissions can administer the whole org
Workspace permissions apply only to API tokens

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because workspace permissions are broader and workbook permissions are more granular. **B** is incorrect because the two scopes are not identical. **C** is incorrect because workbook permissions do not administer the organization. **D** is incorrect because workspace permissions are not limited to APIs. [Permissions and sharing](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/share-content-and-manage-permissions)

questions.stem: Why should a Sigma developer avoid unnecessary cross-joins in workbook modeling?

options.text: They can explode row counts and create expensive warehouse queries

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because unnecessary cross-joins often increase row counts and query cost dramatically. **B** is incorrect because embedding, JWT security, and version tagging are unrelated. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [Join data in Sigma](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/join-data)

questions.stem: Which TWO workbook design choices most appropriate support a polished analyst experience? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use controls that target relevant elements
Organize related pages and reusable navigation clearly
Hide all filters permanently even when needed
Avoid datasets so each page starts from scratch

questions.explanation: **A and B** are correct because clear organization and purposeful controls improve usability and maintainability. **C** is incorrect because permanently hiding needed filters hurts usability. **D** is incorrect because avoiding datasets reduces reuse and consistency. [Workbooks overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/workbooks-overview)

questions.stem: Why should a developer prefer a published dataset over rebuilding the same joins in many workbooks?

options.text: It improves governance and consistency of business logic
It forces every user to become an admin
It removes the need for warehouse credentials

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because published datasets centralize logic and reduce inconsistency. **B** is incorrect because they do not elevate users to admins. **C** is incorrect because warehouse connectivity is still required. **D** is incorrect because filtering remains available. [Datasets overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/create-a-dataset)

questions.stem: Which role is usually most restricted in Sigma in this scenario?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because Viewer is the most limited common user role focused on consumption. **B** is incorrect because Admin is the broadest role. **C** is incorrect because the other choices are not the standard end-user role spectrum. **D** is incorrect because not applicable. [Permissions and sharing](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/share-content-and-manage-permissions)

questions.stem: A developer wants to troubleshoot why a workbook calculation is expensive. What is the most useful first step?

options.text: Inspect the generated query path and simplify filters, joins, or calculations that block efficient pushdown
Publish the workbook to a different workspace only

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because performance troubleshooting starts by understanding the query shape and reducing expensive operations. **B** is incorrect because API token rotation is unrelated. **C** is incorrect because permissions are not the root cause of query complexity. **D** is incorrect because moving workspaces alone rarely fixes the query plan. [Materialization](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/materialization-overview)

questions.stem: What can the Sigma REST API help automate?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because the API supports programmatic management tasks such as exports and content operations. **B** is incorrect because warehouse pruning happens in the source platform. **C** is incorrect because font rendering and notebook kernels are unrelated. [Sigma API documentation](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/api-reference)

questions.stem: What is the main tradeoff of materialization?

options.text: Faster repeated reads at the cost of managing refreshed persisted results
Perfect real-time freshness with no maintenance
Automatic permission inheritance to every external system

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because materialized results improve speed but introduce refresh/staleness considerations. **B** is incorrect because they are not perfectly real-time by default. **C** is incorrect because warehouse cost does not disappear. **D** is incorrect because external permission propagation is unrelated. [Materialization](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/materialization-overview)

questions.stem: What does Sigma version history help project teams do in this scenario?

options.text: Review and restore prior workbook states

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because version history supports review and rollback of workbook changes. **B** is incorrect because it does not materialize data. **C** is incorrect because credential rotation is an admin task. **D** is incorrect because embed tokens are separate. [Workbooks overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/workbooks-overview)

questions.stem: A team wants one date selector to filter multiple workbook elements at once. What should they configure?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because controls can target one or more workbook elements for shared filtering. **B** is incorrect because warehouse roles govern source access. **C** is incorrect because materialization caches results rather than wiring interactions. **D** is incorrect because JWT claims are for embedding contexts. [Workbook controls](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/workbook-controls)

questions.stem: Why is granular sharing generally safer than granting broad workspace access to everyone?

options.text: It limits access to only the content a user actually needs

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because granular sharing supports least-privilege access. **B** is incorrect because cost is a separate concern. **C** is incorrect because sharing does not automatically replace data filtering. **D** is incorrect because embedding still requires secure design. [Permissions and sharing](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/share-content-and-manage-permissions)

questions.stem: Which warehouse platforms can Sigma commonly connect to?

options.text: Cloud data warehouses such as Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, and Databricks

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because Sigma commonly connects to warehouse and database platforms such as Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, and Databricks. **B** is incorrect because it is not limited to local CSVs or Power BI models. **C** is incorrect because external systems are core to Sigma. [Datasets overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/create-a-dataset)

questions.stem: An embedded workbook must apply a tenant-specific filter automatically for each signed-in customer. Which design most appropriate supports this in this scenario?

options.text: Pass user or tenant context through signed embed parameters and user attributes

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because embedding context plus user attributes supports dynamic tenant-aware filtering at scale. **B** is incorrect because disabling controls does not enforce tenant isolation. **C** is incorrect because screenshots are static and insecure. **D** is incorrect because one workbook per row is unmanageable. [Embed analytics with Sigma](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/embed-analytics-overview)

questions.stem: Which Sigma function category is best for expressions such as `DateAdd` and `DateDiff`?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because `DateAdd` and `DateDiff` are date/time functions. **B** is incorrect because lookup functions retrieve matching values. **C** is incorrect because permission and embedding are not calculation categories. **D** is incorrect because not applicable. [Workbooks overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/workbooks-overview)

questions.stem: Which element type is most appropriate for showing one headline KPI such as month-to-date revenue?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because KPI cards summarize a single metric clearly. **B** is incorrect because input tables capture writeback data. **C** is incorrect because join builders define data relationships. **D** is incorrect because API clients are not workbook elements. [Workbooks overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/workbooks-overview)

questions.stem: When is materialization most useful in Sigma?

options.text: When repeated workbook queries are expensive and a cached warehouse table will improve performance
When you want to remove all warehouse dependencies
When you need to manage workspace permissions
When you need to generate JWTs

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because materialization can persist expensive query results to improve responsiveness. **B** is incorrect because Sigma still depends on the warehouse. **C** is incorrect because permissions and JWT generation are separate concerns. **D** is incorrect because not applicable. [Materialization](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/materialization-overview)

questions.stem: What problem does the `Lookup` function primarily solve in Sigma in this scenario?

options.text: Retrieving a related value from another element or result set

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because Lookup supports cross-element value retrieval and row-level matching patterns. **B** is incorrect because JWTs are for embedding. **C** is incorrect because permissions are administered separately. **D** is incorrect because connections are configured outside calculations. [Workbooks overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/workbooks-overview)

questions.stem: What is the purpose of an embed sandbox or test environment?

options.text: To validate embedded experiences safely before production rollout
To replace all workbook permissions permanently
To eliminate the need for source data

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because a sandbox lets teams test embedded analytics safely. **B** is incorrect because it does not replace permission design. **C** is incorrect because source data is still needed. **D** is incorrect because embedding is not converted into an API. [Embed analytics with Sigma](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/embed-analytics-overview)

questions.stem: Which TWO practices most improve governed reuse in Sigma? (Select TWO)

options.text: Rebuild joins independently in every workbook
Centralize common calculations in reusable assets
Give every user full admin rights

questions.explanation: **A and C** are correct because shared datasets and reusable logic reduce duplication and improve consistency. **B** is incorrect because rebuilding joins everywhere increases drift and maintenance cost. **D** is incorrect because full admin rights are unnecessary and risky. [Datasets overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/create-a-dataset)

questions.stem: Which Sigma capability is most useful when an application must export workbook results programmatically on a schedule?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because the REST API supports automated exports and related content operations. **B** is incorrect because version tags, user attributes, and controls serve different purposes. **C** is incorrect because not applicable. [Sigma API documentation](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/api-reference)

questions.stem: Why might a project team tag workbook versions before a release in this scenario?

options.text: To mark known-good milestones for rollback or audit

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because tagging versions helps release management and rollback. **B** is incorrect because JWT signing is separate. **C** is incorrect because input tables and datasets are different features. **D** is incorrect because not applicable. [Workbooks overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/workbooks-overview)

questions.stem: Why would a developer link workbooks together?

options.text: To reuse parameters or navigate users across related analytical experiences

questions.explanation: **A** is correct here because linked workbooks can share context and improve navigation across related content. **B** is incorrect because version history remains available. **C** is incorrect because permissions still matter. **D** is incorrect because datasets serve a different reuse role. [Workbooks overview](https://help.sigmacomputing.com/docs/workbooks-overview)
